title: Unbounded growth of latency tracking list
description:
## Location
python/src/lichen/sim/metrics.py:115

## Description
The _latencies_us list in Metrics class grows without bound as receptions are recorded. Each call to record_reception() appends to this list (line 148). For long-running simulations with many successful deliveries, this list can consume significant memory. Unlike _delivered and _collision_keys which use deduplication, _latencies_us stores every latency value for statistical calculation.

## Failure Scenario
Long-running chaos test simulates 1 million successful packet deliveries over 24 hours. Each reception appends an int (28 bytes on 64-bit Python) to _latencies_us. List grows to ~28MB just for latency data. Combined with _tx_start_times dict growth (no cleanup of completed transmissions), memory usage grows monotonically. Process eventually OOMs or degrades.

## Category
safety
